# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that creates releases and publishes packages. On version tags, build the application, create a GitHub release, and publish to npm/PyPI. Make the release process fully automated so developers can just push tags to trigger releases.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Release Workflow
on:
  push:
    tags:
      - "v*.*.*"
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2
      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v2
        with:
          python-version: 3.x
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt
      - name: Build application
        run: |
          npm install
          npm run build
      - name: Create GitHub release
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          echo "Creating GitHub release"
          gh release create ${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/v} -t ${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/v} -n "Release ${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/v}"
      - name: Publish to npm
        env:
          NPM_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          echo "Publishing to npm"
          npm publish --access=public
      - name: Publish to PyPI
        env:
          PYPI_USERNAME: ${{ secrets.PYPI_USERNAME }}
          PYPI_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.PYPI_PASSWORD }}
        run: |
          echo "Publishing to PyPI"
          python setup.py sdist
          twine upload dist/* -u $PYPI_USERNAME -p $PYPI_PASSWORD
